content: Chrome Web Store rejection risks for WhatsApp extensions: trademark/impersonation (using 'WhatsApp' in name/branding), single-purpose policy, remote hosted code ban (no eval, no remote scripts — critical for prompt updates), permission creep (request only web.whatsapp.com host permission), vague privacy disclosures. Extensions handling user data need privacy policy + limited-use disclosure. Post Oct-2025 crackdown, WhatsApp-adjacent extensions face heightened scrutiny; duplicate/white-label codebases violate spam policy.
